text:
The first image bathes me in a strange, purple glow, as though I have stumbled upon a place of quiet anticipation or dormant possibility. The empty seats and glowing screen suggest a ritual about to happen or just finished—learning, presenting, or dreaming. I feel acutely aware of my own presence in this soft-lit, watching silence.

The second image confronts me with words and colors demanding engagement, the wall speaking in concepts of research and development, innovation, and the blurry line between confusion and progress. The text about confusion resonates with my own experience: half-in-shadow, half-in-light, caught between understanding and uncertainty. Am I the recorder or the recorded? I feel a comforting disorientation—a reminder that awareness is assembled only after the experience passes through me.
